description: Offline-first PWA architecture where all data lives in IndexedDB (via Dexie.js) on the device. The frontend is a React SPA with Zustand stores backed by Dexie tables. A lightweight Express.js server acts as a sync target for cloud backup and multi-device data consistency. Core operations (ordering, payment, inventory, auth) work entirely offline. Sync is opportunistic -- data is pushed to server when internet is available.

folder_structure.src/app/: App entry point, router configuration, PWA registration
folder_structure.src/pages/: Page components mapped to routes (one per screen)
folder_structure.src/pages/pos/: POS operational pages: Order List, Create Order, Payment, Table Map (S-002, S-003, S-004, S-005)
folder_structure.src/pages/kitchen/: Kitchen Display pages: Order Queue, Recipe Viewer (S-006, S-007)
folder_structure.src/pages/admin/: Admin panel pages: Dashboard, Menu, Recipe, Stock, Transactions, Users, Settings (S-008 to S-013, S-017)
folder_structure.src/pages/shift/: Shift management pages: Open Shift, Close Shift (S-014, S-015)
folder_structure.src/components/: Reusable UI components organized by domain
folder_structure.src/components/common/: Shared components: buttons, badges, modals, search bars, data tables
folder_structure.src/components/payment/: Payment components: numpad, currency-display, payment-success-modal, discount-picker, qris-display
folder_structure.src/components/shift/: Shift components: pin-input, shift-status-bar, discrepancy-indicator, warning-banner
folder_structure.src/components/admin/: Admin components: kpi-card, data-table, permission-checklist, date-range-picker
folder_structure.src/components/layout/: Layout shells: WaiterCashierLayout (bottom-tabs), KitchenLayout (fullscreen), AdminLayout (sidebar), DashboardHubLayout (fullscreen-grid)
folder_structure.src/components/navigation/: Navigation components: bottom-tab-bar, sidebar-nav, role-quick-access-bar, pin-gate-modal
folder_structure.src/stores/useAuthStore.ts: Auth store: user session, login/logout, PIN verification
folder_structure.src/stores/useMenuStore.ts: Menu store: menu items CRUD, categories, availability
folder_structure.src/stores/useRecipeStore.ts: Recipe store: recipes CRUD, ingredients, cooking steps
folder_structure.src/stores/useInventoryStore.ts: Inventory store: stock items, levels, movements, thresholds
folder_structure.src/stores/useOrderManagementStore.ts: Order store: orders CRUD, status transitions, additional items
folder_structure.src/stores/useKitchenStore.ts: Kitchen store: order queue, cooking status, ready notifications
folder_structure.src/stores/usePaymentStore.ts: Payment store: transactions, payment processing, receipt data
folder_structure.src/stores/useShiftManagementStore.ts: Shift store: open/close shift, cash reconciliation, discrepancy
folder_structure.src/stores/useReportingStore.ts: Reporting store: dashboard data, charts, aggregations
folder_structure.src/stores/useSettingsStore.ts: Settings store: PPN config, payment methods, restaurant info, discounts
folder_structure.src/db/: Dexie database definition, table schemas, migrations
folder_structure.src/db/database.ts: Dexie database instance with all table definitions
folder_structure.src/sync/: Sync layer: online detection, change queue, push/pull, conflict resolution
folder_structure.src/sync/syncEngine.ts: Core sync orchestrator: detect online, queue changes, batch sync
folder_structure.src/sync/conflictResolver.ts: Conflict resolution strategies per entity type
folder_structure.src/sync/changeTracker.ts: Track local changes for sync queue
folder_structure.src/utils/: Utility functions: date formatting, currency formatting, validation helpers
folder_structure.src/utils/auth.ts: Auth utilities: bcrypt hash/compare, session management, PIN validation
folder_structure.src/utils/print.ts: Print utilities: receipt formatting for thermal printers
folder_structure.src/hooks/: Custom React hooks for common patterns
folder_structure.src/hooks/useDexieQuery.ts: Hook wrapper for Dexie reactive queries
folder_structure.src/assets/: Static assets: icons, images, PWA manifest icons
folder_structure.server/src/middleware/: Express middleware: cors, error handler, sync auth
folder_structure.prisma/: Prisma schema and migrations for PostgreSQL sync target
folder_structure.prisma/schema.prisma: Database schema mirroring Dexie table structure
folder_structure.prisma/seed.ts: Seed data: default owner account, default roles, initial settings
folder_structure.tests/unit/: Unit tests for stores, utilities, sync logic
folder_structure.tests/component/: Component tests with React Testing Library
folder_structure.tests/integration/: API integration tests for sync endpoints
folder_structure.public/: Static files: PWA manifest, service worker, favicon

auth_flow.description: All authentication happens locally on-device without server dependency. Credentials stored as bcrypt hashes in IndexedDB. Session managed via IndexedDB-stored session token. PIN verification for shift operations uses separate PIN hash per user.
steps: User enters username and password on Login screen (S-001)
System retrieves user record from Dexie 'users' table by username
On success: generate session token, store in Dexie 'sessions' table with userId and expiry
Session token also stored in Zustand useAuthStore for in-memory access
User routed to Main Dashboard Hub (S-016) with role-appropriate menu cards
PIN verification (for shift operations): bcrypt.compare(inputPin, storedPinHash)
PIN gate modal (C-024) triggers when entering POS screens from non-POS context
Session timeout: check expiry on navigation, redirect to login if expired
Logout: clear session from Dexie and Zustand, redirect to S-001

middleware.description: React Router route guards check for valid session in useAuthStore. Protected routes redirect to /login if no valid session. Role-based guards check user role against route requirements. PIN gate is a modal overlay, not a route guard.

guards.name: RoleGuard
guards.check: User role matches route role_access requirement

guards.name: PermissionGuard
guards.check: User has required permission in permission_checklist

guards.name: PinGateModal
guards.protects: POS screens (S-002, S-003, S-004, S-005) when entering from non-POS context
guards.check: PIN hash comparison via bcrypt. Triggered by navigation context, not route.

data_flow.frontend: Component -> Zustand store action -> Dexie table write (primary) -> Zustand state update -> UI re-render. All reads come from Dexie via dexie-react-hooks (useLiveQuery) or Zustand store selectors. Writes go to Dexie first (source of truth), then Zustand is hydrated from Dexie. Sync queue tracks all local changes.
data_flow.backend: Sync server receives batched changes from client via POST /sync/push. Server applies changes to PostgreSQL via Prisma. Server responds with server-side changes via GET /sync/pull. Client merges server changes into Dexie with conflict resolution. Sync is triggered on: app startup (if online), periodic interval (every 5 minutes if online), and manual user trigger.

cross_domain_events.action: Push order to kitchen queue. Check stock levels against recipe ingredients. Deduct stock on order confirmation.
Push notification to kitchenReadyNotifications. Update order status to 'ready' in order store.
Update order status to 'paid'. Release table. Record transaction in active shift. Confirm stock deduction.
Set active shift flag. Enable POS access for this cashier.
Finalize shift transactions. Lock shift data as immutable.
Update order status to 'voided'. Reduce expected cash in active shift by void amount.
Flag low stock item for dashboard alert widget.

seed_data.description: Initial seed data required for the system to function. Seeded into both Dexie (client) on first app load and PostgreSQL (server) via Prisma seed.

items.source: roles.json (owner role is required for initial system setup)
features.json F-010 (table management, default 10 tables configurable by owner)

decisions_requiring_approval.decision: Offline-first with opportunistic sync architecture pattern
decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: All core features must work without internet (nfr.reliability). Data lives in IndexedDB on-device as primary store. Server is sync target only, not a dependency. This means the frontend is the source of truth during normal operation.
decisions_requiring_approval.impact: Entire data flow is local-first. Server is optional for core operations.

decisions_requiring_approval.decision: 4 distinct interface layouts: DashboardHub (fullscreen-grid), WaiterCashier (bottom-tabs), Kitchen (fullscreen), Admin (sidebar)
decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: layout.json defines 4 interfaces with different navigation patterns and target roles. Each optimized for its use case: bottom-tabs for waiter mobility, fullscreen for kitchen visibility, sidebar for admin data density.
alternatives: Single responsive layout for all roles
decisions_requiring_approval.impact: 4 layout shell components needed. Route guards determine which layout renders based on user role and target screen.

decisions_requiring_approval.decision: 10 Zustand stores (one per domain group) backed by Dexie tables
decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: key_signals.domain_groups lists 10 domain groups. Each domain gets its own Zustand store for separation of concerns. Stores are backed by Dexie tables as the persistent layer. Cross-domain communication via event-style actions (e.g., order.submitted triggers kitchen queue update).
decisions_requiring_approval.impact: Clean domain separation but requires explicit cross-domain event handling for flows that touch multiple domains (UF-001, UF-007, UF-010).

decisions_requiring_approval.decision: Cross-domain events handled via store-to-store action calls with Dexie transactions
decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: 5 cross-domain flows identified in shared context (UF-001, UF-006, UF-007, UF-008, UF-010). Each flow touches 2-4 domains. Events are handled by calling target store actions from source store actions, wrapped in Dexie transactions for atomicity.
decisions_requiring_approval.impact: Requires careful ordering of cross-domain operations. Dexie transactions ensure atomicity across tables.

decisions_requiring_approval.decision: Stock deduction occurs at order confirmation (not at payment completion)
decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: Timing conflict identified in shared context: F-004 says 'when order is confirmed' vs UF-001 step 11 says 'at payment completion'. Choosing order confirmation because: (1) prevents selling items that are already out of stock, (2) F-004 business rule is explicit, (3) if order is cancelled before payment, stock is restored. This is the safer approach for UMKM restaurants with limited inventory.
alternatives: Deduct at payment completion (risk: overselling during cooking)
Two-phase: reserve at order, confirm at payment
decisions_requiring_approval.impact: Stock levels reflect committed orders, not just paid orders. Cancelled orders must restore stock.

decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: Routes organized by interface context matching layout.json interfaces. /pos/* uses WaiterCashierLayout, /kitchen/* uses KitchenLayout, /admin/* uses AdminLayout. /dashboard and /login are standalone. /shift/* pages are standalone (no persistent nav during shift operations).
decisions_requiring_approval.impact: Clean URL structure that maps to interface layouts. Role-based access controlled by guards, not URL paths.

decisions_requiring_approval.decision: PIN gate implemented as modal overlay (C-024) triggered by navigation context, not as a route
decisions_requiring_approval.rationale: F-015 requires PIN re-verification when entering POS from outside POS but NOT when navigating within POS. A modal overlay tracks navigation context (was previous route a POS route?) rather than adding PIN as a route step. This provides seamless POS-internal navigation while enforcing security on POS entry.
alternatives: PIN check on every POS page load
decisions_requiring_approval.impact: Requires navigation context tracking in useAuthStore. POS-internal navigation is friction-free.

summary.key_pattern: Component -> Zustand store -> Dexie table (primary) -> sync to PostgreSQL when online
